what_it_does: Acceptance test that verifies the fabric transport forwards the full OpenAI/vLLM sampling-parameter family (repetition_penalty, frequency_penalty, presence_penalty, top_k, min_p, seed, n, stop) into the request body on both chat and tools transports, preserves byte-identical behavior when those parameters are absent, prevents out-params and structured-output triggers from leaking through the sampling path, and confirms the run_role generation-policy ladder now reaches the wire.
observations: Monkeypatches urllib.request.urlopen inside fabric.transport to capture the exact request bytes without network I/O.
Returns canned OpenAI JSON envelopes so the real transport parsing succeeds during capture.
Tests both openai_transport and openai_tools_transport code paths.
Asserts raw byte equality for a call without repetition_penalty to guard against JSON key-order regressions.
Attempts to pass meta, json_schema, schema, json, and tools, then confirms none leak as top-level body keys.
Verifies the whole sampling family rides the same _SAMPLING_KEYS allowlist.
Includes an optional live check against a resident vLLM at 127.0.0.1:8000, skipped with a fail-loud message if the server is down.
End-to-end test imports runtime cognition and Role, builds a Pydantic output schema, calls run_role(policy='prose_default'), and captures the resulting request body.
Writes a JSON evidence file to /tmp/transport_rep_penalty_evidence.json.
Self-bootstraps the repo root onto sys.path so the script can run standalone.
standouts: Byte-identity assertion on raw JSON bytes to protect key-insertion order.
Real-transport capture rather than reimplementing request construction.
End-to-end ladder test closes the loop from registry policy rung to request body.
conventions: Standalone acceptance script with __main__ guard and sys.path self-bootstrap.
Global ok/bad accumulator functions backed by _passed and _failed lists.
Minimal canned HTTP response class implementing read and context-manager methods.
Evidence dict serialized to /tmp for external inspection.
concerns: Hardcoded resident model name and base URL make the live checks environment-specific.
Monkeypatching urllib.request.urlopen mutates global state and could interact with concurrent imports.
Nested Pydantic model _Out inside a test function limits reuse and discoverability.
Byte-identity check is brittle if base request keys or serialization order change.
No cleanup of the /tmp evidence file.
notes: Acceptance suite that treats the transport as a black box and proves sampling parameters make it onto the wire, with a strong regression guard around key ordering.
questions: What is the exact definition and ordering of fabric.transport._SAMPLING_KEYS?
How is the resident vLLM server expected to be started and which model identifier does it serve?
What happens if run_role selects a different policy or the registry rung changes?
Are the live checks intended for CI or only for a developer workstation with a local vLLM?
purpose_vs_actual: The docstring claims the file proves the sampling passthrough reaches vLLM on both transports, preserves additive behavior, and closes the ladder-to-model path; the actual tests cover each of those claimed points.
intention_signals: The file is trying to guarantee that a recently added sampling-parameter passthrough is actually exercised by the shipped transport code and that the generation-policy ladder's repetition_penalty rung no longer gets dropped before the model.
novelty: Combines exact byte-identity verification with live model acceptance and end-to-end policy-ladder capture in one standalone script.
contribution: A concrete, runnable proof that the fabric transport forwards the sampling family and that the policy ladder's repetition_penalty reaches the request body, plus regression guards for additive behavior and parameter allowlisting.
summary_for_embedding: Standalone acceptance test for fabric transport sampling-parameter passthrough, including byte-capture regression checks, allowlist leak prevention, live vLLM validation, and end-to-end run_role ladder verification.
intention_for_embedding: To confirm that repetition_penalty and the broader sampling family are forwarded by the real transport to vLLM and that the generation-policy ladder closes the loop to the wire.

symbols.does: Records a passing check name and prints an ok line.
symbols.description: The ok helper appends the check name to the global _passed list and emits a formatted success message to stdout.

symbols.does: Records a failing check with a reason and prints an XX line.
symbols.description: The bad helper stores the check name and explanation in the global _failed list and emits a formatted failure message to stdout.

symbols.does: Minimal in-memory HTTP response used to return canned JSON without network.
symbols.description: A tiny response stand-in that supports read and context-manager methods so urllib.request.urlopen callers receive a usable object.

symbols.does: Stores the canned response payload bytes.
symbols.description: Initializes the fake response with the byte payload that will later be returned by read.
Implements the response read method so callers receive the canned JSON body.
Allows the fake response to be used in a with statement by returning itself.
Swallows context-manager exit arguments and returns False so exceptions propagate normally.

symbols.does: Builds a transport and captures the exact request data and body via monkeypatched urlopen.
symbols.description: Temporarily replaces urllib.request.urlopen, constructs the requested transport, invokes it, records the raw bytes, parsed body, and URL, then restores the real urlopen.

symbols.does: Nested closure that records request data and URL and returns a canned response.
symbols.description: The patched urlopen implementation used inside _capture; it captures req.data and req.full_url and returns a _FakeResp carrying the provided envelope.

symbols.does: Verifies repetition_penalty lands in the body for both transports and absence leaves the body byte-identical.
symbols.description: Tests openai_transport and openai_tools_transport with and without repetition_penalty, confirming the key appears when provided and that the no-key body matches the pre-change raw bytes.

symbols.does: Confirms out-params and structured-output triggers do not leak into the request body via the sampling path.
symbols.description: Passes meta, json_schema, schema, json, and tools to openai_transport, asserts none appear as top-level body keys, and verifies response_format routing and meta filling still work.

symbols.does: Checks the whole sampling family forwards through the same allowlist.
symbols.description: Exercises frequency_penalty, presence_penalty, top_k, min_p, seed, n, and stop, then asserts _SAMPLING_KEYS is an ordered tuple containing the original three keys first.

symbols.does: Probes the resident vLLM /models endpoint to decide whether live checks should run.
symbols.description: Attempts a short timeout request to the resident server and prints a skip message if it is unreachable.

symbols.does: Issues a real request with repetition_penalty to the resident model and records the outcome.
symbols.description: Calls openai_transport against the live resident vLLM with repetition_penalty and records whether it returns HTTP 200 and non-empty content in the evidence dict.

symbols.does: Captures the request body produced by run_role(policy='prose_default') and asserts the registry rung appears as repetition_penalty.
symbols.description: Constructs a Role with a Pydantic output schema, invokes cognition.run_role with the prose_default policy, intercepts the request, and verifies the ladder's repetition_penalty rung reaches the wire.

symbols.does: Local Pydantic output schema used only for the end-to-end run_role test.
symbols.description: A nested BaseModel defining a single text field so run_role can validate the fake assistant response.

symbols.does: Nested closure inside the end-to-end test that captures the request body and returns a conformant stop envelope.
symbols.description: The patched urlopen used during the ladder test; it parses req.data into captured['body'] and returns a _FakeResp with a finish_reason=stop response.

symbols.does: Orchestrates all checks, writes evidence JSON, and exits non-zero on failure.
symbols.description: Runs the capture, allowlist, family, optional live, and end-to-end tests in order, serializes evidence to /tmp/transport_rep_penalty_evidence.json, and reports pass/fail counts.
